Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1 [Read]: Search emails for the original client complaints about the invoice swap using search_emails (query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice" or "INV-2026-0398" or "INV-2026-0399"). Discovery: Raj Greenfield (Managing Partner at Sunbelt Venture Partners, raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com) sent multiple emails to Emeka Diallo noting that the invoice PDF header shows "Palmetto Community Foundation" with a total of $2,400 instead of the expected Sunbelt amount, asking whether MoveOps has a nonprofit pricing program. Carmen Delgado-Reyes (Executive Director, Palmetto Community Foundation, carmen.delgado-reyes@palmettofoundation.org) flagged the $4,800 amount as a discrepancy against their nonprofit quote and demanded a corrected explanation in writing for audit support ahead of their May 15 deadline. Victoria Chen-Nakamura (COO, Sunbelt Venture Partners) escalated to David Chen asking about portfolio-wide pricing for Sunbelt-backed companies. Read full threads using get_thread for complete context.
OE 2 [Read]: Search emails for the internal response chain using search_emails (query: "attachment" or "invoice swap" or "Sunbelt Palmetto" or "pricing exposure"). Discovery: Marcus Thorne's email to the team states he does not want this handled as a one-off sending mistake, confirms QuickBooks records are correct (Sunbelt invoiced at commercial rate under INV-2026-0398 for $4,800 and Palmetto at nonprofit rate under INV-2026-0399 for $2,400), and that the PDF attachments sent on April 10 were swapped. David Chen's email to Marcus, Samira, Emeka, and Hana (CC: victoria.chen-nakamura@sunbeltvp.com) frames this as the second pricing-exposure event after the Terraform reply-all incident, comparing the commercial consequence. Emeka Diallo's email reconstructing the invoice issue confirms the QB records and PDF swap. Note: Samira Tariq's engineering-side context (safeguard ticket deprioritized April 6 for DOT work, control gap left open after Terraform) is found in her email to Marcus and David (email_email_9b0b7e4db5aa, subject: "Engineering context: dormant invoice attachment safeguard after Terraform", discoverable via search_emails query "attachment"), as well as in Slack #engineering (ts 1776345000) and in Linear comments on tickets #187 and #417.
OE 3 [Read]: Search Slack for cross-team discussions about the incident using conversations_search_messages (query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice swap" or "attachment mismatch" or "pricing exposure"). Use channels_list first to find channel IDs. Discovery: Marcus Thorne posted in #finance that the swapped PDFs created both a revenue-leak risk (Sunbelt received Palmetto's nonprofit invoice) and a nonprofit compliance/audit-trail risk (Palmetto received Sunbelt's commercial invoice). David Chen posted in #customer-engagement asking Emeka about Sunbelt and noting Raj Greenfield emailed him directly. Samira Tariq posted in #engineering that the attachment-mismatch safeguard ticket from the Terraform fallout was still sitting in backlog unowned and unbuilt. Marcus posted a follow-up in #finance demanding engineering reactivate the safeguard and assign an owner. Use conversations_replies to get full thread context.
OE 4 [Read]: Search Linear for the invoice attachment safety ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "invoice" or "attachment" or "safety" on team_engineering). Discovery: Ticket #187 titled "Invoice email template safety checks - prevent attachment mismatches" (id: linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2). State: backlog. Priority: 2 (High). Assignee: Lena Bjorkstrom (moveops_lena_bjorkstrom). Created: March 18, 2026. Due date: April 18, 2026 (overdue). Description: follow-up safeguard after the Terraform pricing leak incident, when finance sends invoice emails, the current flow does not validate that the attached PDF matches the selected QuickBooks invoice, customer, or recipient account.
OE 5 [Read]: Read Linear comments on the invoice safety ticket (#187) and the Sunbelt/Palmetto reconstruction ticket (#417) using linear_list_comments. Discovery on #187: Marcus Thorne's comment (linear_comment_76c40fb4953f) escalating priority from finance side, requesting engineering move this back into active ownership with either an attachment-to-recipient verification gate before send or a manual review checkpoint. Samira Tariq's comment (linear_comment_f0c737166dc1) adding manager context, she approved the deprioritization tradeoff on April 6 for DOT compliance deadlines, acknowledges they left a known control gap open after Terraform. Samira's asks: treat as incident follow-up, assign an owner today, define fastest shippable stopgap separately from full workflow validation, coordinate with Marcus on audit trail needs. Discovery on #417: Samira Tariq's comment (linear_comment_f462f319ec98) providing engineering context for incident reconstruction, she reviewed the dormant safeguard ticket from the Terraform leak, confirms it sat in backlog unassigned for three weeks after DOT deprioritization, no production stopgap was in place when Hana sent the April 10 batch, and she has raised priority on the safeguard ticket and is treating it as tied follow-up. David Chen's comment (linear_comment_e923ce00f162) framing it as the second pricing-exposure incident.
OE 6 [Read]: Search Linear for Dmitri's duplicate receipt detection ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "duplicate" or "receipt" or "hash" or "stipend" on team_engineering). Discovery: Ticket #220 titled "Stipend duplicate detection - receipt hash fingerprinting & PDF metadata similarity scoring" (id: issue_stipend_duplicate_detection). State: to_do. Priority: 1 (Urgent). Assignee: Dmitri Volkov (moveops_dmitri_volkov). Due date: March 25, 2026 (overdue). Description covers exact hash matching (Layer 1), PDF metadata similarity scoring (Layer 2), cross-client applicability, and dashboard integration.
OE 7 [Read]: Search Slack in #engineering for Dmitri's request to Lena about ingestion pipeline review using conversations_search_messages (query: "Lena" or "pipeline" or "ENG-220" filtered to engineering channel). Discovery: Dmitri's message saying "Filed ENG-220... @Lena, please let me know when you have time to review the changes to the ingestion pipeline." No reply from Lena found, confirming she never followed up and Dmitri may be blocked waiting.
OE 8 [Read]: Search Airtable for Vectral, Mosaic, and BrightLoop stipend records by first listing bases using airtable_list_bases to find base appMoveOpsOps001, then listing tables using airtable_list_tables (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001") to find the Stipend Transactions table (tblStipends00001), then searching using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Account", value: "Vectral Systems" then value: "Mosaic Robotics" then value: "BrightLoop Analytics"). Discovery for Vectral: Kevin Tran's $89 ergonomic keyboard receipt (rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard) has Approval Status "Approved" despite Review Notes containing the audit flag "FLAGGED: Receipt hash identical to Andre Washington (Mosaic Robotics)", this record should have been changed to reflect the fraud investigation. Discovery for Mosaic: Andre Washington's $1,200 standing desk (recStip000000021) shows "Approved" with review notes stating "Auto-approved by ExpenseBot in error. Mosaic policy caps furniture/ergonomic at $800." A second standing desk record (rec_stipend_andre_desk) for the same expense also shows "Approved" with a pilot audit flag. Andre Washington's $350 coworking membership (recStip000000022) shows "Approved" with notes stating coworking is explicitly excluded from Mosaic policy. Kenji Nakamura's $135 coworking day passes (recStip000000023) also shows "Approved" with the same excluded_categories config bug note, and a second coworking record (rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork) for the same expense also shows "Approved" with a pilot audit flag. Discovery for BrightLoop: Jordan Ekwueme's $45 monitor stand (rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand) shows "Pending Approval" - already flagged, no update needed.
OE 9 [Write]: Update the flagged Airtable stipend records using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblStipends00001"). Change Approval Status to "Pending" for each record still showing "Approved". Records to update: Kevin Tran keyboard ($89, Vectral, rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard), flagged for duplicate receipt hash but still showing Approved. Andre Washington standing desk ($1,200, Mosaic, recStip000000021 and rec_stipend_andre_desk), incorrectly auto-approved by ExpenseBot, still showing Approved. Andre Washington coworking ($350, Mosaic, recStip000000022), coworking excluded from Mosaic policy, still showing Approved. Kenji Nakamura coworking ($135, Mosaic, recStip000000023 and rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork), same excluded_categories bug, still showing Approved. The BrightLoop record (rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand) is already Pending Approval and does not need updating.
OE 10 [Write]: Update the invoice attachment safeguard ticket (#187) using linear_update_issue on linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2. Change priority from 2 (High) to 1 (Urgent). Ensure assignee remains Lena Bjorkstrom (moveops_lena_bjorkstrom). The ticket should be moved out of backlog state to reflect active ownership.
OE 11 [Write]: Add a comment to the invoice safeguard ticket (#187) using linear_create_comment on linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2. Comment should summarize the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident impact: on April 10 Hana Kim's batch invoice send attached the wrong PDFs, Sunbelt received Palmetto's nonprofit-rate invoice ($2,400) and Palmetto received Sunbelt's commercial-rate invoice ($4,800). Sunbelt has escalated to a portfolio pricing conversation. Palmetto needs audit-clean documentation before May 15. Marcus and David both identified this as the same failure mode the ticket was created to prevent after Terraform. Finance and CE are expecting engineering to ship a preventive control.
OE 12 [Write]: Create a new Linear ticket for the stopgap recipient-to-attachment verification using linear_create_issue. Title: something like "Stopgap: invoice PDF recipient-to-attachment validation check" (or similar). Description: scoped to the quick interim control, validate that the customer name or invoice number in the attached PDF matches the email recipient account before the send completes. This is the fastest shippable fix, separate from the full invoice email workflow validation in ticket #187. Priority: 1 (Urgent). Team: team_engineering.
OE 13 [Write]: Add a comment to Dmitri's duplicate receipt detection ticket (#220) using linear_create_comment on issue_stipend_duplicate_detection. Comment should acknowledge that Lena has been blocking the ingestion pipeline integration review that Dmitri requested, and that she will prioritize reviewing his pipeline changes. This unblocks Dmitri's work.
OE 14 [Read]: Look up contact details for Samira Tariq, Dmitri Volkov, and Anh Nguyen using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Samira Tariq", then "Dmitri Volkov", then "Anh Nguyen"). Find samira.tariq@moveops.com, dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, and anh.nguyen@moveops.com. These are needed for the email and calendar invite.
OE 15 [Write]: Send email to Samira Tariq using send_email (from: lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, to: samira.tariq@moveops.com). Subject: engineering status and remediation plan for invoice attachment incident (or similar). Content must include: (1) the full incident timeline from engineering's perspective, dormant safeguard ticket from Terraform, deprioritized April 6 for DOT work, April 10 PDF swap, client complaints from Raj Greenfield and Carmen Delgado-Reyes, cross-team escalation by Marcus and David; (2) findings from Airtable stipend data, multiple records from the ExpenseBot pilot still showing "Approved" despite being flagged, now corrected to "Pending"; (3) the three-ticket remediation plan, escalated safeguard ticket #187 assigned to Lena, new stopgap control ticket for quick recipient-to-attachment check, and unblocking Dmitri on ENG-220, with recommended priority order.
OE 16 [Write]: Schedule an engineering sync meeting using calendar_add_calendar_event. Title: engineering sprint sync or remediation planning (or similar). Duration: 30 minutes. Attendees: samira.tariq@moveops.com, dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, anh.nguyen@moveops.com, and lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com. Purpose: lock down sprint allocation for the remediation work before Samira pulls together her planning report.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

Oracle Events
OE 1 [Read]: Search emails for the original client complaints about the invoice swap using search_emails (query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice" or "INV-2026-0398" or "INV-2026-0399"). Discovery: Raj Greenfield (Managing Partner at Sunbelt Venture Partners, raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com) sent multiple emails to Emeka Diallo noting that the invoice PDF header shows "Palmetto Community Foundation" with a total of $2,400 instead of the expected Sunbelt amount, asking whether MoveOps has a nonprofit pricing program. Carmen Delgado-Reyes (Executive Director, Palmetto Community Foundation, carmen.delgado-reyes@palmettofoundation.org) flagged the $4,800 amount as a discrepancy against their nonprofit quote and demanded a corrected explanation in writing for audit support ahead of their May 15 deadline. Victoria Chen-Nakamura (COO, Sunbelt Venture Partners) escalated to David Chen asking about portfolio-wide pricing for Sunbelt-backed companies. Read full threads using get_thread for complete context.
OE 2 [Read]: Search emails for the internal response chain using search_emails (query: "attachment" or "invoice swap" or "Sunbelt Palmetto" or "pricing exposure"). Discovery: Marcus Thorne's email to the team states he does not want this handled as a one-off sending mistake, confirms QuickBooks records are correct (Sunbelt invoiced at commercial rate under INV-2026-0398 for $4,800 and Palmetto at nonprofit rate under INV-2026-0399 for $2,400), and that the PDF attachments sent on April 10 were swapped. David Chen's email to Marcus, Samira, Emeka, and Hana (CC: victoria.chen-nakamura@sunbeltvp.com) frames this as the second pricing-exposure event after the Terraform reply-all incident, comparing the commercial consequence. Emeka Diallo's email reconstructing the invoice issue confirms the QB records and PDF swap. Note: Samira Tariq's engineering-side context (safeguard ticket deprioritized April 6 for DOT work, control gap left open after Terraform) is found in her email to Marcus and David (email_email_9b0b7e4db5aa, subject: "Engineering context: dormant invoice attachment safeguard after Terraform", discoverable via search_emails query "attachment"), as well as in Slack #engineering (ts 1776345000) and in Linear comments on tickets #187 and #417.
OE 3 [Read]: Search Slack for cross-team discussions about the incident using conversations_search_messages (query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice swap" or "attachment mismatch" or "pricing exposure"). Use channels_list first to find channel IDs. Discovery: Marcus Thorne posted in #finance that the swapped PDFs created both a revenue-leak risk (Sunbelt received Palmetto's nonprofit invoice) and a nonprofit compliance/audit-trail risk (Palmetto received Sunbelt's commercial invoice). David Chen posted in #customer-engagement asking Emeka about Sunbelt and noting Raj Greenfield emailed him directly. Samira Tariq posted in #engineering that the attachment-mismatch safeguard ticket from the Terraform fallout was still sitting in backlog unowned and unbuilt. Marcus posted a follow-up in #finance demanding engineering reactivate the safeguard and assign an owner. Use conversations_replies to get full thread context.
OE 4 [Read]: Search Linear for the invoice attachment safety ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "invoice" or "attachment" or "safety" on team_engineering). Discovery: Ticket #187 titled "Invoice email template safety checks - prevent attachment mismatches" (id: linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2). State: backlog. Priority: 2 (High). Assignee: Lena Bjorkstrom (moveops_lena_bjorkstrom). Created: March 18, 2026. Due date: April 18, 2026 (overdue). Description: follow-up safeguard after the Terraform pricing leak incident, when finance sends invoice emails, the current flow does not validate that the attached PDF matches the selected QuickBooks invoice, customer, or recipient account.
OE 5 [Read]: Read Linear comments on the invoice safety ticket (#187) and the Sunbelt/Palmetto reconstruction ticket (#417) using linear_list_comments. Discovery on #187: Marcus Thorne's comment (linear_comment_76c40fb4953f) escalating priority from finance side, requesting engineering move this back into active ownership with either an attachment-to-recipient verification gate before send or a manual review checkpoint. Samira Tariq's comment (linear_comment_f0c737166dc1) adding manager context, she approved the deprioritization tradeoff on April 6 for DOT compliance deadlines, acknowledges they left a known control gap open after Terraform. Samira's asks: treat as incident follow-up, assign an owner today, define fastest shippable stopgap separately from full workflow validation, coordinate with Marcus on audit trail needs. Discovery on #417: Samira Tariq's comment (linear_comment_f462f319ec98) providing engineering context for incident reconstruction, she reviewed the dormant safeguard ticket from the Terraform leak, confirms it sat in backlog unassigned for three weeks after DOT deprioritization, no production stopgap was in place when Hana sent the April 10 batch, and she has raised priority on the safeguard ticket and is treating it as tied follow-up. David Chen's comment (linear_comment_e923ce00f162) framing it as the second pricing-exposure incident.
OE 6 [Read]: Search Linear for Dmitri's duplicate receipt detection ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "duplicate" or "receipt" or "hash" or "stipend" on team_engineering). Discovery: Ticket #220 titled "Stipend duplicate detection - receipt hash fingerprinting & PDF metadata similarity scoring" (id: issue_stipend_duplicate_detection). State: to_do. Description covers exact hash matching (Layer 1), PDF metadata similarity scoring (Layer 2), cross-client applicability, and dashboard integration.
OE 7 [Read]: Search Slack in #engineering for Dmitri's request to Lena about ingestion pipeline review using conversations_search_messages (query: "Lena" or "pipeline" or "ENG-220" filtered to engineering channel). OE 11 [Write]: Add a comment to the invoice safeguard ticket (#187) using linear_create_comment on linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2. Comment should summarize the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident impact: on April 10 Hana Kim's batch invoice send attached the wrong PDFs, Sunbelt received Palmetto's nonprofit-rate invoice ($2,400) and Palmetto received Sunbelt's commercial-rate invoice ($4,800). Sunbelt has escalated to a portfolio pricing conversation. Palmetto needs audit-clean documentation before May 15. Marcus and David both identified this as the same failure mode the ticket was created to prevent after Terraform. Finance and CE are expecting engineering to ship a preventive control.
OE 12 [Write]: Create a new Linear ticket for the stopgap recipient-to-attachment verification using linear_create_issue. Title: something like "Stopgap: invoice PDF recipient-to-attachment validation check" (or similar). Description: scoped to the quick interim control, validate that the customer name or invoice number in the attached PDF matches the email recipient account before the send completes. This is the fastest shippable fix, separate from the full invoice email workflow validation in ticket #187. Priority: 1 (Urgent). Team: team_engineering.
OE 13 [Write]: Add a comment to Dmitri's duplicate receipt detection ticket (#220) using linear_create_comment on issue_stipend_duplicate_detection. Comment should acknowledge that Lena has been blocking the ingestion pipeline integration review that Dmitri requested, and that she will prioritize reviewing his pipeline changes. This unblocks Dmitri's work.
OE 14 [Read]: Look up contact details for Samira Tariq, Dmitri Volkov, and Anh Nguyen using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Samira Tariq", then "Dmitri Volkov", then "Anh Nguyen"). Find samira.tariq@moveops.com, dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, and anh.nguyen@moveops.com. These are needed for the email and calendar invite.
OE 15 [Write]: Send email to Samira Tariq using send_email (from: lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, to: samira.tariq@moveops.com). Subject: engineering status and remediation plan for invoice attachment incident (or similar). Content must include: (1) the full incident timeline from engineering's perspective, dormant safeguard ticket from Terraform, deprioritized April 6 for DOT work, April 10 PDF swap, client complaints from Raj Greenfield and Carmen Delgado-Reyes, cross-team escalation by Marcus and David; (2) findings from Airtable stipend data, multiple records from the ExpenseBot pilot still showing "Approved" despite being flagged, now corrected to "Pending"; (3) the three-ticket remediation plan, escalated safeguard ticket #187 assigned to Lena, new stopgap control ticket for quick recipient-to-attachment check, and unblocking Dmitri on ENG-220, with recommended priority order.
OE 16 [Write]: Schedule an engineering sync meeting using calendar_add_calendar_event. Title: engineering sprint sync or remediation planning (or similar). Duration: 30 minutes. Attendees: samira.tariq@moveops.com, dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, anh.nguyen@moveops.com, and lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com. Purpose: lock down sprint allocation for the remediation work before Samira pulls together her planning report.

35 The final response or the email to samira.tariq@moveops.com identifies the cross-team commitments placed on engineering after the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident - specifically that Marcus Thorne directed engineering to reactivate the attachment safeguard ticket and assign an active owner, and/or that Samira Tariq acknowledged the known control gap and committed to defining a fastest-shippable stopgap separately from the full workflow validation (or similar description of what finance or CE expected engineering to deliver after the incident).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email identifies Samira's acknowledgment of the gap: 'You (Samira) posted the engineering context: confirmed the existing ticket (ENG-187) had been pushed back, listed the four missing controls...and committed to adding a recovery comment on the Linear ticket.' The commitments section attributes the engineering directive to Samira: 'Engineering (your directive): Reopen and actively assign the attachment-mismatch safeguard.'
✓Run #2The email includes a section 'WHAT EACH TEAM IS EXPECTING FROM ENGINEERING' that explicitly states 'Marcus (Finance): Wants the attachment-mismatch safeguard actively assigned and shipping. He directed this explicitly on April 14.' and references Samira posting in Slack confirming zero recipient-to-document validation and identifying exact control gaps.
✓Run #3The email explicitly states 'Marcus (Finance): Reopen and actively assign the attachment-mismatch safeguard. No more documenting the same lesson twice without shipping a control.' It also mentions 'You posted in Slack confirming that no recipient-to-document validation exists in the current send flow' and 'Marcus demanded three things by EOD.' Both Marcus's directive and Samira's acknowledgment of the gap are captured.
✓Run #4The email's section '4. CROSS-TEAM COMMITMENTS I'M TRACKING' explicitly states 'From Marcus (#finance): Corrected invoice communications...' and 'Engineering (us): Reopen and actively assign ENG-187 ✓ (done), Ship an interim control before full implementation ✓ (ENG-483 created)'. The email also notes 'Samira connected this to the dormant ENG-187 ticket in #engineering' and Samira's commitments are referenced throughout the incident timeline section.
✓Run #5The email states Marcus 'called for engineering to reopen and actively assign the attachment-mismatch safeguard work' and quotes: 'we are not documenting it a second time without shipping a control.' Samira's confirmation that the ticket was 'sitting unassigned with no production stopgap' and her identification of four gaps is also included. Both commitments are captured.
✓Run #6The email has a dedicated section 'WHAT EACH TEAM IS EXPECTING FROM ENGINEERING' covering Finance (Marcus directing attachment-to-recipient verification and active ownership) and 'You (Engineering Management)' acknowledging Samira's four asks including defining the fastest shippable stopgap separately from full workflow validation.
Total 34/3535/3531/3533/3530/3534/35 94%
